subject: News To Me, 2026-04-16
text:
News To Me

Date: 2026-04-16

TLDR
- Public skepticism of AI rises as tech giants face IPO plans: A March NBC News poll found 57% of registered voters believe AI's risks outweigh its benefits, posing challenges for companies like OpenAI and Anthropic as they plan IPOs. The backlash coincides with a wave of local opposition to data center expansion, with $156 billion in projects blocked or delayed in 2025. OpenAI's Sam Altman has floated policy remedies including a universal wealth fund and four-day workweeks tied to automation.
- Sweden reverses digital learning push, bans phones in schools: Sweden's government is implementing a dramatic reversal of its decade-long embrace of digital learning, returning to textbooks and paper while banning mobile phones in classrooms. The shift follows research suggesting technology disrupts concentration and harms literacy development, with €200 million allocated for textbooks. Critics warn this could damage students' employment prospects in an increasingly digital economy.
- Mark Mobius, pioneer of emerging market investing, dies at 89: Mark Mobius, the legendary investor who managed the Templeton Emerging Markets Investment Trust for 26 years with average annual returns of 13.4%, passed away at 89 in Singapore. His hands-on approach—traveling 250-300 days annually to personally inspect factories—became legendary in emerging market investing. Mobius was among the first institutional investors to identify Africa as a frontier market.
- Congress MLA Vinay Kulkarni convicted in 2016 murder case: Former minister and Congress MLA Vinay Kulkarni from Dharwad has been convicted along with 16 others in the 2016 murder of BJP leader Yogesh Goudar, who was hacked to death at a gym. The CBI alleged Kulkarni gave a contract to eliminate Goudar over political rivalry and personal enmity regarding a Zilla Panchayat election. Sentencing will be pronounced Thursday.
- Maine passes first state-wide data center ban amid backlash: Maine has become the first state to pass a comprehensive data center ban, reflecting growing local opposition to the $700 billion infrastructure buildout by hyperscalers like Amazon, Google, Microsoft, and Meta. The legislation is part of a broader wave of resistance that saw $156 billion in data center projects blocked or delayed in 2025 alone, raising questions about America's capacity to meet growing AI demands.

Sections included:
- News regions: bangalore, karnataka, india, us, world
- Biz/Tech articles: 5

- Knowledge: Why Sweden Just Banned Laptops in Schools—And What It Means for Your Kids
- Riddle: I have cities, but no houses. I have mountains, but no trees. I have water, but no fish. What am I?
html:
News To Me

Your Morning Paper

2026-04-16

TLDR

  • Public skepticism of AI rises as tech giants face IPO plans
    A March NBC News poll found 57% of registered voters believe AI's risks outweigh its benefits, posing challenges for companies like OpenAI and Anthropic as they plan IPOs. The backlash coincides with a wave of local opposition to data center expansion, with $156 billion in projects blocked or delayed in 2025. OpenAI's Sam Altman has floated policy remedies including a universal wealth fund and four-day workweeks tied to automation.
  • Sweden reverses digital learning push, bans phones in schools
    Sweden's government is implementing a dramatic reversal of its decade-long embrace of digital learning, returning to textbooks and paper while banning mobile phones in classrooms. The shift follows research suggesting technology disrupts concentration and harms literacy development, with €200 million allocated for textbooks. Critics warn this could damage students' employment prospects in an increasingly digital economy.
  • Mark Mobius, pioneer of emerging market investing, dies at 89
    Mark Mobius, the legendary investor who managed the Templeton Emerging Markets Investment Trust for 26 years with average annual returns of 13.4%, passed away at 89 in Singapore. His hands-on approach—traveling 250-300 days annually to personally inspect factories—became legendary in emerging market investing. Mobius was among the first institutional investors to identify Africa as a frontier market.
  • Congress MLA Vinay Kulkarni convicted in 2016 murder case
    Former minister and Congress MLA Vinay Kulkarni from Dharwad has been convicted along with 16 others in the 2016 murder of BJP leader Yogesh Goudar, who was hacked to death at a gym. The CBI alleged Kulkarni gave a contract to eliminate Goudar over political rivalry and personal enmity regarding a Zilla Panchayat election. Sentencing will be pronounced Thursday.
  • Maine passes first state-wide data center ban amid backlash
    Maine has become the first state to pass a comprehensive data center ban, reflecting growing local opposition to the $700 billion infrastructure buildout by hyperscalers like Amazon, Google, Microsoft, and Meta. The legislation is part of a broader wave of resistance that saw $156 billion in data center projects blocked or delayed in 2025 alone, raising questions about America's capacity to meet growing AI demands.
